rule_name: Missing Road Marking — Visibility Compliance Failure
standard: Saudi Highway Code SHC 602 (Saudi MUTCD) | MOMAH Urban Roads Maintenance Obligations
recommended_action: Inspect per Saudi Highway Code SHC 602 (Saudi Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices). Road markings must be visible day and night and incorporate retroreflective properties using glass beads in thermoplastic or approved materials. MOMAH municipalities are responsible for rehabilitating faded or missing ground markings on urban roads. Schedule immediate repainting using compliant thermoplastic retroreflective material. Report via the Balady platform if within municipal jurisdiction.
ai_recommendation: The road inspection AI has identified a high-severity safety issue due to missing road markings, which poses a significant visibility compliance failure. This issue increases the risk of accidents as road users may struggle to navigate lanes or intersections properly, especially under low-light conditions or adverse weather. Immediate corrective action is required to repaint the missing road markings to ensure clear guidance for drivers and enhance overall road safety. Prompt attention to this matter is crucial to mitigate potential hazards and align with Saudi MOMAH and KSARAP safety standards.

standard: SASO 2927:2019 (mandatory since September 2020)
recommended_action: Install additional street light. Gap exceeds SASO 2927:2019 maximum 40m spacing for collector/arterial roads. Verify pole height and lux output meet ME-class illuminance requirements.
ai_recommendation: The road inspection AI has identified a street light spacing violation, which poses a medium-level safety risk with a risk score of 49 out of 100. Inadequate lighting can compromise visibility for drivers and pedestrians, increasing the likelihood of accidents, particularly during nighttime or adverse weather conditions. It is recommended to reassess the current spacing of street lights in the affected area and adjust them according to Saudi MOMAH and KSARAP standards to ensure optimal illumination. This corrective action should be prioritized to enhance road safety and prevent potential incidents.

recommended_action: Report via the Balady platform (balady.gov.sa) under MOMAH General Specifications for Urban Roads Construction. The responsible municipality or contracted maintenance entity is legally obligated to inspect and repair the road surface defect. Unaddressed potholes causing vehicle damage or accidents may result in legal liability for the contractor or municipal authority under Saudi law. Commission immediate pavement assessment and schedule urgent repair works.

Given the risk score of 50 out of 100, this corrective action should be prioritized to maintain road safety standards in compliance with Saudi MOMAH and KSARAP guidelines.

rule_name: Construction Waste Obstruction on Public Road
standard: MOMAH Requirements for Disposal of Construction, Demolition & Renovation Waste | MWAN (National Center for Waste Management) Regulations | Municipal Violations Penalty Regulations
recommended_action: Report under MOMAH Requirements for the Disposal of Construction, Demolition, and Renovation Waste. Dumping construction waste on public roads is a municipal violation carrying fines of up to SAR 50,000. Contractors are legally obligated to transport all waste to designated disposal sites using licensed vehicles. Non-removal of debris after completing works attracts additional fines of SAR 3,000–10,000. Coordinate with the municipality to issue a compliance notice to the responsible contractor and enforce immediate site clearance.

This obstruction poses a significant safety risk as it can lead to accidents by impeding the normal flow of traffic and reducing visibility for drivers. Immediate corrective action is required to remove the construction waste and ensure the road is clear and safe for public use. Prompt attention to this matter is crucial to prevent potential accidents and maintain road safety standards in compliance with Saudi MOMAH and KSARAP guidelines.

standard: MOMAH Municipal Regulations on Abandoned Vehicles | Saudi Traffic Law (Moroor) | Absher Platform Enforcement
recommended_action: Report per MOMAH municipal regulations on abandoned vehicles. Vehicles left on public roads for more than seven days are subject to impoundment by municipal or traffic authorities (Moroor). Owner is notified via Absher platform. If the vehicle is causing active road obstruction or safety hazard, contact Moroor (911) for immediate removal. Vehicle owner is liable for towing and storage fees plus applicable traffic violation fines. Ensure the scene is marked with warning signs until the obstruction is cleared.

rule_name: Ambulance Entrance — Missing Advance Warning Signs
standard: SHC 602 (Saudi MUTCD) — Section 7: Emergency Facility Signage
recommended_action: Install emergency sign series at 100m and 50m upstream of ambulance entrance per SHC 602. Signs must be retroreflective and illuminated.
ai_recommendation: The AI has identified a critical safety issue: an ambulance entrance is missing advance warning signs. This poses a high risk as emergency vehicles may not be identified in time, delaying critical care delivery.

standard: SASO 2927:2019 | SHC 602 Emergency Access Lighting
recommended_action: Install dedicated street lighting at ambulance entrance per SASO 2927:2019 emergency access requirements. Minimum illuminance: 30 lux at road surface.
ai_recommendation: The AI has detected an ambulance entrance with inadequate or missing street lighting. This is a critical safety issue that can delay emergency response and increase accident risk at the access point.
